Off Calendar Nov-01-2019 Continued To Jan-08-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-07-2019 Overruled
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Maria Bradish, Esq. 800-506-2652 of the Moore Law Group for Plaintiff through Court Call.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MICHAEL LIPSON's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

Defendant Michael E. Lipson's demurrer to Plaintiff Discover Bank's complaint is overruled. Plaintiff sufficiently pleads the three elements of a common count. (Compl. CC-1.a, CC-1.b, & CC-2; see Farmers Ins. Exchange v. Zerin (1997) 53 Cal.App.4th 445, 460 ["The only essential allegations of a common count are (1) the statement of indebtedness in a certain sum; (2) the consideration-i.e., goods sold, work done, etc.; and (3) nonpayment."].) A common count is not a specific cause of action, but rather a simplified form of pleading normally used to state the existence of monetary indebtedness. (McBride v. Boughton (2004) 123 Cal.App.4th 379, 394.) Therefore, "it has long been settled the allegation of claims using common counts is good against special or general demurrers." (Farmers Ins. Exchange, 53 Cal.App.4th at 460.)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

Off Calendar Oct-01-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Maria Bradish, Esq. 800-506-2652 of the Moore Law Group, A.P.C. for Plaintiff through Court Call.
Timothy Rumberger, Esq. 510-841-5500 for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant argues to be paid reasonable fees caused by filing a fraudulent proof of service. Plaintiff responds. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT MICHAEL LIPSON'S Motion To Set Aside Default Judgment And Request For Sanctions.

Taken Under Submission.

Defendant to submit a proposed order.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
